questions.text: What does the Surah teach about human beginnings and humility?
questions.answer: Humans were once nothing worth mentioning, so arrogance is irrational.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that humans are naturally self-sufficient and independent.
questions.answer: False. It emphasizes dependence on Allah from creation onward.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about human moral choice?
questions.answer: Allah showed the path; people choose gratitude or ingratitude.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Insan describe the righteous’ motivation for charity?
questions.answer: They feed others purely for Allah’s pleasure and do not seek thanks or reward.

questions.text: What is one practical way to give charity with this intention today?
questions.answer: Give quietly, avoid reminding people, and focus on Allah’s acceptance rather than people’s praise.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about fulfilling vows and commitments?
questions.answer: The righteous fulfill vows as part of integrity and fear of the Hereafter.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests feeding the needy is only recommended when convenient.
questions.answer: False. It praises feeding even difficult cases like orphans and captives.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Insan teach about the Day that is feared by the righteous?
questions.answer: It is a Day of widespread hardship, motivating sincere preparation and mercy to others.

questions.text: How does the Surah connect inner sincerity with outward social ethics?
questions.answer: Sincere hearts show mercy, feed others, fulfill vows, and remain patient in worship.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah protecting the righteous from the evil of that Day?
questions.answer: Allah grants them radiance and joy, showing salvation is a gift of mercy.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe Paradise in a way that motivates the heart?
questions.answer: It describes comfort, beauty, honored drink, and peace—without worldly harms like extreme heat or cold.

questions.text: What is one lesson from the Surah’s mention of Paradise clothing and adornment?
questions.answer: Allah honors the righteous with dignity and beauty, replacing worldly struggle with comfort.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that comfort in Paradise is only spiritual, not described in real terms.
questions.answer: False. It describes comfort in dignified, tangible imagery.

questions.text: What does the 'pure drink' teach about Allah’s reward?
questions.answer: Allah’s reward is purified from harm and is a special honor from the Lord.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach the Prophet about patience with people who reject truth?
questions.answer: Be patient for Allah’s decree and do not obey persistent sinners and ingrates.

questions.text: How can believers apply this patience guidance in modern life?
questions.answer: Do not let social pressure drag you into sin; stay firm and patient with Allah’s timing.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about remembering Allah morning and evening?
questions.answer: Regular remembrance anchors faith and keeps the heart from dunya’s distractions.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that remembering Allah is only for special religious people.
questions.answer: False. It encourages remembrance as a daily habit for believers.

questions.text: Why do disbelievers turn away according to this Surah’s message?
questions.answer: Because they love immediate life and neglect the heavy Day of accountability.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Insan teach about human will and Allah’s will?
questions.answer: Human choices exist, but they occur within Allah’s overarching will and knowledge.

questions.text: How does this teaching protect a believer from despair and arrogance?
questions.answer: It builds humility (Allah controls) and hope (Allah can guide and help).

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that sincerity means never enjoying Allah’s blessings.
questions.answer: False. It praises enjoying reward with gratitude, while keeping motives for Allah.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the dignity of serving others even when they cannot repay you?
questions.answer: Serving purely for Allah is a high moral rank, free from ego and dependence on people’s thanks.

questions.text: What is one practical habit to build ‘feeding for Allah’s face’ (wajh Allah)?
questions.answer: Regularly support one person or charity without telling anyone and without expecting recognition.

questions.text: What is one daily habit you can change using Surah Al-Insan’s themes (gratitude, sincerity, patience, charity)?
questions.answer: For example: start a small, consistent secret charity; replace complaints with gratitude; or add a daily reminder and dhikr habit to stay sincere and patient.

questions.text: What does the Surah say about the time before man was 'a thing mentioned'?
questions.answer: That there was a long period of time when man was nothing worth mentioning.

questions.text: From what was man created?
questions.answer: From a mixture of sperm-drops (Nutfatin Amshajin).

questions.text: What two paths has Allah shown to mankind?
questions.answer: The path of gratitude (Shakir) and the path of ingratitude (Kafur).

questions.text: What does this Surah say Allah has prepared for the disbelievers?
questions.answer: Chains, shackles, and a blaze.

questions.text: What is the drink of the righteous in Paradise?
questions.answer: A cup mixed with camphor (Kafur).

questions.text: What is 'Aynan' (a spring) from which the servants of Allah drink?
questions.answer: A spring they cause to gush forth as they will.

questions.text: List three characteristics of the righteous in this Surah.
questions.answer: They fulfill their vows, they fear a Day of widespread evil, and they feed the poor, orphans, and captives.

questions.text: Why do the righteous feed the needy?
questions.answer: They say: 'We feed you only for the countenance of Allah. We wish not from you reward or gratitude'.

questions.text: What will Allah protect the righteous from on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The evil of that Day, and He will grant them radiance and happiness.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the climate of Paradise?
questions.answer: They will see therein neither excessive heat (sun) nor intense cold.

questions.text: How are the vessels of Paradise described?
questions.answer: Silver vessels and crystal-clear cups of silver, determined by measure.

questions.text: What is the drink mixed with 'Zanjabil' (Ginger)?
questions.answer: A drink from a spring named 'Salsabil'.

questions.text: Who will circulate among the inhabitants of Paradise?
questions.answer: Immortal youths; if you saw them, you would think them scattered pearls.

questions.text: What will the dwellers of Paradise wear according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Green garments of fine silk and brocade, and they will be adorned with silver bracelets.

questions.text: What is the 'Pure Drink' (Sarabun Tahura) given by their Lord?
questions.answer: A drink given as a reward for their striving.

questions.text: What is the command to the Prophet (SAW) regarding patience?
questions.answer: Be patient for the decision of your Lord and do not obey among them any sinner or ungrateful one.

questions.text: When does this Surah encourage remembering Allah?
questions.answer: Morning and afternoon, and during the night by prostrating and exalting Him.

questions.text: Can anyone will something except by the will of Allah?
questions.answer: No, 'you do not will except that Allah wills'.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His mercy and the wrongdoers at the end?
questions.answer: He admits whom He wills into His mercy; for the wrongdoers He has prepared a painful punishment.
